message:
fix(safety): harden integer arithmetic with saturating ops to prevent overflow panics

Replace bare += 1, .sum(), and unchecked casts with saturating_add,
saturating_sub, saturating_mul, and try_from across five engine modules:

- activation_lifecycle: all 8 transition_count increments use saturating_add
- alloc_domain: allocation_sequence increment and total_used/total_capacity
  aggregation switched from .sum() to .fold() with saturating_add to prevent
  Add-chain panics
- closure_model: next_handle increment uses saturating_add; ClosureHandle
  constructor uses u32::try_from().unwrap_or(u32::MAX) instead of silent
  truncating cast
- offline_synthesis_pipeline: enum domain bound calculation uses
  saturating_sub and saturating_mul for cardinality * 1_000_000
- parser: string escape index advance uses saturating_add for defense-in-depth
  (already clamped by .min(bytes.len()))

These sites could theoretically wrap on extreme inputs; saturating to MAX
is strictly safer than panicking or silently truncating.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
